payload.reason: EU regulatory domain (NL/528/ETSI) prohibits UNII-3 channels 149-165 for indoor APs. ch149 cannot be configured. AP03 left on ch100. Playbar unchanged. No production impact.

payload.fix: Add control-plane=controller-manager to the dragonfly-operator pod template labels via the bjw-s defaultPodOptions.labels in the HelmRelease. Flux reconciles, the operator pod gets the label, can probe :9999, elects a master, applies role=master label to one pod, the dragonfly Service endpoint populates, and authelia recovers.
payload.risk_notes: Medium because auth-affecting + adds a pod-spec label, but no API surface or RBAC changes. Operator restart is benign — the StatefulSet is healthy.
payload.rollback: git revert the helmrelease commit; flux reconciles back. Operator returns to the same (broken) state but cluster is no worse off than now.
payload.root_cause: dragonfly-operator (deployed via bjw-s app-template) emits a NetworkPolicy on the Dragonfly CR that allows port 9999 (admin port) ingress only from pods labeled control-plane=controller-manager. The bjw-s app-template gives the operator pod app.kubernetes.io/* labels but NOT that kubebuilder label, so the operator cannot reach :9999 on the dragonfly pods to query dataset-load status and elect a master. Result: no pod gets role=master, the dragonfly Service has no endpoints, authelia cannot connect to redis at 10.43.135.100:6379 and crashloops.

payload.correction: FIRST PUT (data_rate_kbps=12000 + advertising_rates=true, but preference left at auto) was SILENTLY CLAMPED by the controller. GET-after-PUT (T1 attempt 1) showed advertising_rates took (false->true) but data_rate_kbps REVERTED to 1000 on all 4 SSIDs — classic UniFi silent-field-drop. Root cause: minrate_setting_preference=auto makes the controller recompute/ignore the manual data_rate_kbps. This is the exact footgun the verify-before-report rule guards against; rc=ok was a false positive.
payload.delta_to_record: 10.4.57 wlanconf min-rate model: to set a MANUAL 2.4GHz floor you MUST set minrate_setting_preference=manual; with it on auto the data_rate_kbps field is silently clamped to the computed default (1000). advertising_rates is honored independently of preference.

payload.lesson_applied: 2026-06-13 move-shutdown: setting nodown while client I/O was still active caused OSD PGs to block (512 slow ops, node01 drain hung). Fix that worked = scale CLIENTS down FIRST. This plan quiesces all client workloads BEFORE touching any Ceph flag, and only sets nodown once clients are quiesced.

rollback.abort_is_safe: Up to STEP 3 everything is reversible: flux resume, scale workloads back up, CNPG un-hibernate, ceph osd unset all flags. Cluster returns to current green state.
rollback.bringup_reverse: power network -> CP -> workers -> ceph GATE 2 (mons+osds up) -> unset flags (nodown,norecover,nobackfill,norebalance,noout) -> GATE 3 active+clean -> flux resume -> un-hibernate CNPG -> scale workloads back up -> Hestia re-enable 4 automations.
rollback.if_clients_wont_quiesce: Do NOT set nodown. Investigate stuck writer; if a StatefulSet won't drain, leave it and abort the shutdown rather than force flags with active I/O (that is exactly the 2026-06-13 failure mode).
rollback.if_pgs_block_after_flags: If slow ops / blocked PGs appear (the 512-slow-op signature), STOP - do not power nodes. Unset nodown, let I/O settle, escalate to Atlas.

sequence: STEP 0 (parallel, Hestia): HA safe-state - pause the 4 automations, record they were ON (rack-relocation Hestia A1).

STEP 1 SCALE CLIENTS DOWN FIRST (Heph): (a) flux suspend all app Kustomizations + HelmReleases so nothing re-reconciles workloads back up; (b) scale app Deployments/StatefulSets to 0 across media/home-automation/tools/etc; (c) CNPG hibernate postgres16 (clean primary shutdown, NOT delete); (d) scale Dragonfly, influxdb, pgadmin to 0. GOAL: zero client I/O to Ceph before any flag.
STEP 2 CONFIRM I/O QUIESCED (Heph): ceph status shows client io ~0 op/s; no rbd/cephfs writers left. Only THEN proceed.

STEP 3 CEPH QUIESCE FLAGS (Heph, runbook Heph §2): ceph osd set noout; norebalance; nobackfill; norecover; then nodown LAST and only because clients are now quiesced. Confirm flags listed in ceph status. NO ceph osd pause.

STEP 4 GRACEFUL NODE SHUTDOWN (OPERATOR, runbook Heph §3): talosctl shutdown WORKERS first (.81 .82 .83) then CP last (.84 .85 .86). Run from OPERATOR terminal - talosctl port 50000 is firewalled from this sandbox (verified: no route to host).
STEP 5 NETWORK LAST (Iris, runbook Iris §4): only after all 6 nodes + NAS confirmed off, power network edge-inward.

payload.plan: New small app kubernetes/main/apps/observability/monitoring-orphan-scan/ (own lifecycle/RBAC, does NOT touch the kube-prometheus-stack HelmRelease). Daily CronJob (03:37 Europe/Amsterdam, off-peak/off-:00; image docker.io/bitnamilegacy/kubectl:1.33.4 already used by the envoy status-keeper CronJob, ships kubectl+jq) runs scan.sh: list live Helm release secrets (sh.helm.release.v1.<rel>.vN, owner=helm) in observability, list ServiceMonitors+PrometheusRules, then jq set-difference flags any with managed-by=Helm or instance label AND zero ownerReferences AND instance NOT in the live-release set. Emits ONE JSON line {orphan_monitoring_count:N, orphans:[{name,kind,namespace,instance,age}], scan_namespace}. promtail/Loki ingest obs stdout. Loki ruler alert MonitoringOrphanResources added to the existing loki-rules ConfigMap (the single mounted ruler source) fires severity=warning when count>0 over 25h, routed to the same kube-prometheus-stack Alertmanager->HA path as the other Loki-ruler alerts.
payload.rbac: NAMESPACED Role+RoleBinding in observability ONLY. Read-only: get/list on monitoring.coreos.com servicemonitors+prometheusrules, and get/list on core secrets. NO ClusterRole, NO cluster-wide secret read, NO write/patch/delete anywhere.
